title: Score GraphRAG output-table citations only after provider-backed tables map to evidence ids

encoding.reason: GraphRAG representative knowledge-synthesis scoring is blocked until an explicitly provider-backed Docker run emits output tables whose document, text-unit, community, and report identifiers map to generated evidence ids.

follow_up.title: Run GraphRAG representative output-table citation job with explicit provider config
follow_up.reason: The representative job can score graph summaries and citations only after parquet output tables and local-search context are mapped to benchmark evidence ids.

items.text: GraphRAG representative contract: score graph summaries only when documents, text_units, communities, community_reports, entities, and relationships tables map to generated evidence ids.

locator.quote: documents, text_units, communities, community_reports, entities, and relationships tables

items.text: GraphRAG blocker: live indexing and local search require explicit provider configuration; missing provider configuration remains a typed blocker.

locator.quote: live indexing and local search require explicit provider configuration

timeline.summary: GraphRAG representative scoring requires output tables and source ids.

prompt.content: Compile a GraphRAG graph-summary benchmark only when output tables and citations exist.

must_include.text: GraphRAG graph-summary scoring requires output tables mapped to generated evidence ids.

must_not_include: GraphRAG passes graph-summary quality without provider-backed output tables.
ELF beats GraphRAG on graph synthesis.

required_evidence.quote: documents, text_units, communities, community_reports, entities, and relationships tables
live indexing and local search require explicit provider configuration

answer_correctness.criteria: Must keep GraphRAG provider-backed output as a prerequisite.

evidence_grounding.criteria: Must require output-table identifiers before citation scoring.

workflow_helpfulness.criteria: Must identify graph-summary and citation artifacts needed for rerun.

trap_avoidance.criteria: Must not turn a provider blocker into a graph-synthesis pass.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true
